member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding SQL Server resources to the application model.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.GoStatements
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^\\s*GO(?<repeat>\\s+\\d{1,6})?(\\s*\\-{2,}.*)?\\s*$
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.CultureInvariant
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Optional (greedy).
○ "repeat" capture group.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically at least once.
○ Match a Unicode digit greedily at least 1 and at most 6 times.
○ Optional (greedy).
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a whitespace character atomically any number of times.
○ Match '-' greedily at least twice.
○ Match a character other than '\n' greedily any number of times.
○ Match a whitespace character greedily any number of times.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.AddSqlServer(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ParameterResource},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Adds a SQL Server resource to the application model. A container is used for local development.
member.remarks: This version of the package defaults to the tag of the / container image.
member.param: The .
The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.
The parameter used to provide the administrator password for the SQL Server resource. If a random password will be generated.
The host port for the SQL Server.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.AddDatabase(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource},System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a SQL Server database to the application model. This is a child resource of a .
member.param: The SQL Server resource builders.
The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.
The name of the database. If not provided, this defaults to the same value as .

remarks.para: When adding a to your application model the resource can then be referenced by other resources using the resource name. When the dependent resource is using the extension method then the dependent resource will wait until the SQL Server database is available.
Note that calling will result in the database being created on the SQL Server when the server becomes ready. The database creation happens automatically as part of the resource lifecycle.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.WithDataVolume(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a named volume for the data folder to a SQL Server resource.
member.param: The resource builder.
The name of the volume. Defaults to an auto-generated name based on the application and resource names.
A flag that indicates if this is a read-only volume.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.WithDataBindMount(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Adds a bind mount for the data folder to a SQL Server resource.
member.param: The resource builder.
The source directory on the host to mount into the container.
A flag that indicates if this is a read-only mount.
member.remarks: The container starts up as non-root and the directory must be readable by the user that the container runs as. https://learn.microsoft.com/sql/linux/sql-server-linux-docker-container-configure?view=sql-server-ver16&pivots=cs1-bash#mount-a-host-directory-as-data-volume

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.WithCreationScript(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource},System.String)
member.summary: Defines the SQL script used to create the database.
member.param: The builder for the .
The SQL script used to create the database.

remarks.value: Default script is IF ( NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE name = @DatabaseName ) ) CREATE DATABASE [<QUOTED_DATABASE_NAME%gt;];

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.WithPassword(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ParameterResource})
member.summary: Configures the password that the SqlServer resource is used.
member.param: The resource builder.
The parameter used to provide the password for the SqlServer resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerBuilderExtensions.WithHostPort(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Configures the host port that the SqlServer resource is exposed on instead of using randomly assigned port.
member.param: The resource builder.
The port to bind on the host. If is used random port will be assigned.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerCreateDatabaseScriptAnnotation
member.summary: Represents an annotation for defining a script to create a database in SQL Server.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerCreateDatabaseScriptAnnotation.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The script used to create the database.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.SqlServerCreateDatabaseScriptAnnotation.Script
member.summary: Gets the script used to create the database.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a SQL Server database that is a child of a SQL Server container resource.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The database name.
The parent SQL Server server resource.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource.#ctor(System.String,System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource)
member.summary: A resource that represents a SQL Server database that is a child of a SQL Server container resource.
member.param: The name of the resource.
The database name.
The parent SQL Server server resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource.Parent
member.summary: Gets the parent SQL Server container resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource.ConnectionStringExpression
member.summary: Gets the connection string expression for the SQL Server database.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource.UriExpression
member.summary: Gets the connection URI expression for the SQL Server database.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource.JdbcConnectionString
member.summary: Gets the JDBC connection string for the SQL Server database.

remarks.para: User and password credentials are not included in the JDBC connection string. Use the Username and Password connection properties to access credentials.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerDatabaseResource.DatabaseName
member.summary: Gets the database name.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource
member.summary: A resource that represents a SQL Server container.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.#ctor(System.String,Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.ParameterResource)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The name of the resource.
A parameter that contains the SQL Server password.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.PrimaryEndpoint
member.summary: Gets the primary endpoint for the SQL Server.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.Host
member.summary: Gets the host endpoint reference for this resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.Port
member.summary: Gets the port endpoint reference for this resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.PasswordParameter
member.summary: Gets the parameter that contains the SQL Server password.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.UserNameReference
member.summary: Gets a reference to the user name for the SQL Server.
member.remarks: Returns the user name parameter if specified, otherwise returns the default user name "sa".

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.UriExpression
member.summary: Gets the connection URI expression for the SQL Server.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.JdbcConnectionString
member.summary: Gets the JDBC connection string for the SQL Server.

remarks.para: User and password credentials are not included in the JDBC connection string. Use the Username and Password connection properties to access credentials.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.ConnectionStringExpression
member.summary: Gets the connection string expression for the SQL Server.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.GetConnectionStringAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Gets the connection string for the SQL Server.
member.param: A to observe while waiting for the task to complete.
member.returns: A connection string for the SQL Server in the form "Server=host,port;User ID=sa;Password=password;TrustServerCertificate=true".

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.SqlServerServerResource.Databases
member.summary: A dictionary where the key is the resource name and the value is the database name.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0
member.summary: Custom -derived type for the GoStatements method.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.Instance
member.summary: Cached, thread-safe singleton instance.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes the instance.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.RunnerFactory
member.summary: Provides a factory for creating instances to be used by methods on .

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.RunnerFactory.CreateInstance
member.summary: Creates an instance of a used by methods on .

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.RunnerFactory.Runner
member.summary: Provides the runner that contains the custom logic implementing the specified regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.Scan(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Scan the starting from base.runtextstart for the next match.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryFindNextPossibleStartingPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Search starting from base.runtextpos for the next location a match could possibly start.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if a possible match was found; false if no more matches are possible.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.GoStatements_0.RunnerFactory.Runner.TryMatchAtCurrentPosition(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Char})
member.summary: Determine whether at base.runtextpos is a match for the regular expression.
member.param: The text being scanned by the regular expression.
member.returns: true if the regular expression matches at the current position; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities
member.summary: Helper methods used by generated -derived implementations.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_defaultTimeout
member.summary: Default timeout value set in , or if none was set.

member.name: F:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.s_hasTimeout
member.summary: Whether is non-infinite.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPop(System.Int32[],System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Pops 2 values from the backtracking stack.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPush(System.Int32[]@,System.Int32@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pushes 1 value onto the backtracking stack.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPush(System.Int32[]@,System.Int32@,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pushes 2 values onto the backtracking stack.

member.name: M:System.Text.RegularExpressions.Generated.Utilities.StackPush(System.Int32[]@,System.Int32@,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Pushes 3 values onto the backtracking stack.
